title: The Lifecycle of E-Waste and its Impact on Oceanic Ecosystems
description: A visual trace of electronic waste from improper disposal to its pathways into marine environments, illustrating its detrimental effects on different trophic levels.
relationships: Leachate from landfills contaminates groundwater and rivers, which flow into the ocean
Toxins (e.g., mercury, lead) are absorbed by plankton, entering the base of the food web
Bioaccumulation concentrates toxins in the tissues of smaller organisms
Biomagnification increases toxin concentration at each successive trophic level, reaching dangerous levels in top predators and eventually humans
